name: Reaching the Age of Adolescence
subject: biology
summary: Adolescence (11–19 years) — puberty, hormones, reproductive health, and personal hygiene during this transition.

crushed.title: Adolescence = 11 to 19 years
crushed.text: Transition from childhood to adulthood. Body and mind both change rapidly.

crushed.title: Puberty = physical changes
crushed.text: Triggered by hormones. Voice changes, height spurt, hair growth, reproductive organs mature.

crushed.title: Hormones control it all
crushed.text: Testosterone (male), Oestrogen (female), Growth hormone, Insulin — chemical messengers in blood.

crushed.title: Reproductive health matters
crushed.text: Balanced diet, exercise, hygiene. Say no to drugs — they damage liver, brain, and reproductive system.

crushed.title: Personal hygiene
crushed.text: More sweat & oil during puberty — bathe daily, clean clothes, oral hygiene.

items.label: Height increase
items.detail: Sudden growth spurt — long bones grow rapidly

items.label: Voice change
items.detail: Boys: voice deepens as larynx grows. Girls: voice slightly deeper

items.label: Body hair
items.detail: Underarms, pubic region, face (boys) — androgen hormones trigger this

items.label: Reproductive maturity
items.detail: Testes/ovaries mature — capable of producing gametes

items.label: Emotional changes
items.detail: Mood swings, increased independence, peer influence

items.label: Growth hormone
items.detail: Pituitary gland — controls height and overall growth

compare.title: Secondary Sexual Characters — Boys vs Girls

flows.title: How a Hormone Works
flows.description: Endocrine gland releases hormone → travels in blood → acts on target organ.

steps.label: Stimulus
steps.description: E.g. blood sugar rises after eating.

steps.label: Gland
steps.description: Endocrine gland (e.g. pancreas) secretes hormone into blood.

steps.label: Bloodstream
steps.description: Hormone carried to target organs throughout body.

steps.label: Target organ
steps.description: Target cells have receptors — change their activity.

steps.label: Balance restored
steps.description: Body returns to normal — hormone release slows down.

keyTerms.definition: Period between childhood and adulthood (approx. 11–19 years)
keyTerms.analogy: The upgrade phase between kid and adult software

keyTerms.definition: Stage when body becomes capable of reproduction
keyTerms.analogy: When your body switches on adult mode

keyTerms.definition: Chemical messenger secreted by endocrine glands into blood
keyTerms.analogy: A text message sent through your bloodstream

keyTerms.definition: Monthly shedding of uterus lining in females
keyTerms.analogy: A monthly reset of the uterus preparation system

demos.title: Endocrine Hormone Signal
demos.description: A gland releases a hormone that travels in blood to a target organ.

steps.label: Gland activated
steps.description: Brain signal triggers hormone release from endocrine gland.

steps.label: Hormone in blood
steps.description: Hormone travels through bloodstream to distant organs.

steps.label: Target responds
steps.description: Target organ cells receive signal — growth or change begins.

chapterSummary.overview: Adolescence is the transition from childhood to adulthood (roughly 11–19 years), marked by puberty. This chapter covers physical and emotional changes, the role of hormones, reproductive health, personal hygiene, and balanced nutrition during this critical growth phase.
keyPoints: Puberty brings secondary sexual characters — voice change, height spurt, acne, body hair.
Hormones (testosterone, estrogen) from endocrine glands control these changes.
Reproductive health: awareness of AIDS (HIV), personal hygiene, and responsible behaviour.
Balanced diet with iron, calcium, and protein supports rapid growth.
Adolescents need exercise, adequate sleep, and emotional support from family.
chapterSummary.examFocus: List changes during puberty. Explain how hormones work. Discuss hygiene and health tips for adolescents.
